overview: From 2026-06-27 to 2026-06-28, elizaos/eliza had 36 new PRs (8 merged), 1 new issues, and 7 active contributors.

topIssues.title: Cloud apps: multi-tenant hardening & completion before GA (audit work order)
topIssues.author: NubsCarson
topIssues.body:
## Cloud apps — multi-tenant hardening & completion before GA

Work order from a deep audit of the **apps / container-deploy / per-tenant-DB** path. The architecture is solid and well-tested in pieces; this is the checklist to take it from *"staging canary (allowlisted orgs)"* to *"safe for self-serve external users."*

**Ground rules for whoever picks this up (cloud agent):**
- Implement + verify **everything in staging** first. The container-deploy and DB-isolation paths only fully exercise when the **provisioning node daemon is armed** — arm it in staging before testing.
- Each item below: re-confirm the exact file:line in the current tree before changing (the audit was a snapshot).
- Land as small, reviewable PRs grouped by the sections below. P1 items are the gate before opening to non-allowlisted users.

---

### P1 — security gates (do first; required before any external / self-serve user)

1. **Rate limiting everywhere it's missing** — global, anonymous-session minting, and the metered inference paths; add sybil control on the free-credit grant. *Today these are unthrottled, so a loop against the anon/inference path is unbounded cost.* **Effort: M**
2. **Enforce the reserved/managed env-var denylist on BOTH container-deploy routes.** The denylist exists but is only applied on one route; the other lets a caller's `environmentVars` override managed keys (e.g. the metered-identity / cloud token). Strip reserved keys on both. **Effort: S**
3. **Centralize outbound fetch behind one IP-pinned SSRF guard** (`safeFetch` that pins the resolved IP for the actual request). Current validate-then-fetch is TOCTOU → DNS-rebinding pivot into the cloud/metadata network. **Effort: M**
4. **Close the Worker-path "isolated DB" silent fallback.** Isolated-DB only truly isolates on the node-daemon path (real `CREATE ROLE`/`REVOKE CONNECT`); the pure-Worker path silently falls back to a shared DB (isolation by UUID only). Either finish the per-tenant DDL TODO in the Worker path, or **hard-fail "isolated" when the daemon isn't armed** — never silently degrade to shared. **Effort: M–L**
5. **Digest-pin the production image allowlist** and tighten the default GHCR orgs — no trailing-`*` namespace trust, no mutable `:latest`. **Effort: S**
6. **CI gate against unscoped tenant DB reads** + run the existing isolation-verify script in CI. Tenant isolation currently rests on every handler remembering to pass `organization_id`; one slip = cross-tenant read. **Effort: S–M**

> The first three (1–3) are the hard gate. Do not open beyond the org allowlist until they're in.

### P2 — completion (make the gated path actually real)

7. **Arm + health-monitor the provisioning daemon as a hard dependency** (alert if down). The container-stop billing cleanup AND real DB isolation both silently break when it isn't running. **Effort: M**
8. **Wire Caddy ingress into the deploy success criteria** so a deploy cannot report success without a reachable public URL. **Effort: S–M**
9. **One end-to-end staging test of the full user chain**: create app → build → isolated DB → live URL. Today it's only covered in disconnected pieces; the "user's own code" build leg is least exercised. **Effort: L**
10. **Finish the consumer payout/redemption side** (currently "temporarily unavailable") and **replace the Cloudflare registrar dev stub** before charging for custom domains in prod. These are live money paths that charge but don't fully deliver yet. **Effort: M**

### P3 — cleanup

11. **Env kill-switch for feature flags** (they're hardcoded `enabled: true` with no runtime override — no way to disable a feature mid-incident). **Effort: S**
12. **Confirm/re-enable the promo duplicate-redemption guard** on the free-credit path (currently commented out → repeat bonus possible). **Effort: S**
13. **Refresh stale comments** (the "no build service yet" header is now wrong) and **re-audit the public-path prefix allowlist**, especially `/api/internal` and `/api/webhooks` that lean on per-handler secret checks (one mis-scoped `startsWith` exposes a subtree). **Effort: S–M**

---

**Bottom line:** strong, well-architected foundation — atomic credit deduction, hashed keys, a real (design-correct) per-tenant DB isolation, prebuilt-image deploy with quota/billing, good unit/integration coverage. The gap is the distance between *"works on the armed-daemon path in staging"* and *"safe for arbitrary external users."* P1 closes that gap.

topPRs.title: test(handoff): e2e the success switch — mock import store + provision-to-running (PR6)
topPRs.author: standujar
topPRs.body:
Stacked on #9846 (PR4).

PR6 of the seamless-provision handoff — the **e2e**: make the SUCCESS handoff reachable + asserted in the cloud e2e harness. Previously the handoff e2e was unreachable (the mock control-plane served no `/conversations/import` route, and the existing spec asserted the handoff does NOT happen).

## How
- **cloud-mocks control-plane** (`server.ts` + `store.ts`): add a mock `/conversations/import|messages` store with idempotent skip-all import (mirrors the real agent route `conversation-routes.ts:1623`).
- **New `app-client-handoff-success.spec.ts`**: creates a shared front, seeds a 4-message transcript, provisions a separate dedicated to `running`, runs the **real** `startCloudAgentHandoff`, and asserts the success contract — `status==="switched"`, `imported===4`, `onSwitch` fired with the dedicated base, transcript copied in order, conversation id stable, + idempotent replay (re-run inserts 0).
- **`cloud-api-dev.mjs`**: an **e2e-scoped** `ELIZA_CLOUD_AGENT_BASE_DOMAIN` override nulling the unreachable dedicated subdomain so the reachable `bridge_url` wins in the local mock stack (gated by `isE2eTestMode`, no prod leak).

## What this e2e CAUGHT
It surfaced a **load-bearing prod bug** the unit tests (which mock the create) couldn't: `POST /agents` hardcoded `reuseExistingNonTerminal: true`, so create-both's 2nd create returned the shared instead of a separate dedicated → the handoff never actually fired in prod. **Fixed in #9854** (forceCreate). This is exactly why the e2e earns its keep.

## Tests
Ran green: `app-client-handoff-success` → `1 passed`, "imported 4/4 message(s)". Passed /clean + /review (PASS).

## Follow-up
With #9854 (forceCreate) landed, this spec can be updated to drive the **real** create-both path (instead of inserting the dedicated row directly) — proving the full prod path e2e.

topPRs.title: fix(computeruse): repair macOS window listing; complete #9581 on-device CUA input coverage (9/9)
topPRs.author: lalalune
topPRs.body:
## What

Completes the first remaining item of #9581 — **macOS on-device CUA verification to full input coverage** — and fixes the two real macOS bugs that were blocking it.

The macOS desktop CUA evidence harness was stuck at **6/9** with `accessibilityPermission`, `mouseKeyboardInput`, and `windowListFocus` reported as `requires_device_evidence`. They were **not** actually blocked on a permission — `AXIsProcessTrusted` / System Events `UI elements enabled` were `true` the whole time. They were blocked by two genuine bugs in `windows-list.ts`.

## Root causes + fixes (`plugins/plugin-computeruse/src/platform/windows-list.ts`)

1. **macOS window listing was 100% broken.** `listWindowsDarwin` / `getActiveWindow` read `(id of w as text)`, but System Events `window` elements have **no `id`** — reading it throws `-1728`/`-1700` for every window, the surrounding `try` swallowed it, and `listWindows()` returned `[]` on every macOS host. So `get_window_position` / `switch_to_window` failed with "Window not found", which the harness mis-attributed to missing Accessibility.
   - Now enumerated via `CGWindowListCopyWindowInfo` (Swift, ~0.5s incl. compile; needs only Screen Recording for titles) with a System Events fallback for hosts without the Swift toolchain. Windows are identified by title scoped to the owning app — the only term the process-level AppleScript targeting can act on.

2. **`runDarwinWindowScript` timed out + was mis-classified.** It walked `every window of` **every non-matching process** through the accessibility tree before reaching the target; on a busy desktop that blew the 5s `osascript` timeout, and `ETIMEDOUT` matches the accessibility-error classifier → bogus "Accessibility denied".
   - Now a two-pass match: process name first (fast, no AX walk), window title only as fallback.

## Harness robustness (`scripts/capture-macos-desktop-evidence.mjs`)

Directly after synthetic keyboard input, TextEdit's **application** Apple Event dispatch (`get text of front document`) is transiently blocked and hangs to its timeout — even though the typed text is already present in the **accessibility tree**. The input proof now reads the text-area AX `value` (exactly what a CUA agent observes on screen), keeping the document read only as a fallback; the cleanup close retries past the same transient block so runs don't leak stale windows.

## Evidence — 9/9 `passed`

Captured on Apple **M4 Max** (`Mac16,5`, macOS `26.2` build `25C56`):

```
bun run --cwd plugins/plugin-computeruse capture:macos-desktop-evidence   # status: passed
bun run --cwd plugins/plugin-computeruse validate:platform-evidence -- \
  ../../.github/issue-evidence/9581-macos-desktop-cua/macos-desktop-validation.json --require-complete
# → [macos-desktop-evidence] 9 checks validated (passed)
```

All nine checks pass; `accessibilityPermission`, `mouseKeyboardInput`, `windowListFocus` flip to `passed`. Artifacts under `.github/issue-evidence/9581-macos-desktop-cua/` include a **screen recording** (`input-proof.mp4`) of the agent driving a controlled TextEdit window (`mouse_move → click → key_combo(cmd+a) → type`, AX-verified) plus the validated manifest/report and `NOTES.md`.

## Tests

- `bun run --cwd plugins/plugin-computeruse typecheck` — clean.
- `bun run --cwd plugins/plugin-computeruse test` — green; added `parseDarwinWindowOutput` regression tests guarding the owner|||title parse so the empty-list bug can't silently return.

Refs #9581.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

topPRs.title: fix: wrap cloud connectors settings section
topPRs.author: odilitime
topPRs.body:
## Summary
- add a CloudConnectorsSettingsSection adapter for the in-app Settings registry
- keep the standalone dashboard/settings/connections cloud route on the raw CloudConnectorsSection because CloudRouterShell already provides query and cloud i18n there
- register the Settings section with the adapter so useCloudT resolves under CloudI18nProvider

## Verification
- bun run --cwd eliza/packages/ui typecheck (run from /Users/user1/Sites/milady3, passed)
- bunx vitest run apps/app/test/package-mode-aliases.test.ts (Milady integration regression, passed)
- bunx @biomejs/biome check apps/app/test/package-mode-aliases.test.ts eliza/packages/ui/src/cloud/connectors/index.ts (passed)
- git diff --check origin/develop...HEAD (clean branch, passed)

Milady integration PR: https://github.com/milady-ai/milady/pull/2200

topPRs.title: fix(cloud): IP-pinned safeFetch to close SSRF/DNS-rebinding window (#9853 P1.3)
topPRs.author: NubsCarson
topPRs.body:
**#9853 P1 — item 3.** One IP-pinned `safeFetch` to close the TOCTOU/DNS-rebinding window: `assertSafeOutboundUrl` validated DNS then returned a *hostname* URL, and ~9 sinks `fetch()`'d it (re-resolving at connect → pivot into `169.254.169.254`/`10.x`/headscale mesh).

- New `security/safe-fetch.ts`: resolves+validates DNS once (reusing the refactored `outbound-url` validators — no duplicate SSRF logic), and on **Node** pins the TCP socket to the validated IP via an `http(s)` `lookup` hook that re-runs `isForbiddenIpAddress` (Host header + TLS servername stay the hostname). Manual redirect handling re-validates + re-pins **every hop**.
- **Honest caveat:** on workerd, `fetch()` can't pin an arbitrary IP — it re-validates immediately before each manual fetch; the residual Worker-side window is documented in `canPinSockets()`. Node sinks (provisioning webhooks run in the daemon) get true pinning.
- Migrated the genuine validate-then-fetch-same-URL sinks (mcp-upstream-forward, provisioning webhook, media-utils, app-promotion, seo, waifu-webhook). **Deliberately left:** content-safety/seo-IndexNow/ad-media (validate-only — the URL is fetched by a downstream provider, not locally) and `eliza-sandbox` (~20 sites that *intentionally* target private mesh/docker IPs — safeFetch rejects private IPs by design; needs a mesh-aware allowlist refactor → separate follow-up).

**Reviewer caveats (from self-review):** no socket-level integration test (not runnable in CI — validation rejects loopback; coverage is at the seams: lookup hook, per-hop re-resolution, fail-closed); `nodePinnedFetch` reimplements fetch over `node:http(s).request` — scrutinize stream body wiring + bodyless-status Response construction (CI typecheck is the gate); `redirect:'error'` now throws our Error vs the platform TypeError (callers handle generically). Pure redirect/lookup logic validated standalone.

Refs #9853 (P1 item 3). The `eliza-sandbox` trusted-private-IP SSRF hardening is a recommended separate item.

topPRs.title: feat(handoff): silent in-place re-point on switch — no flash, no WS drop, no draft loss (PR3)
topPRs.author: standujar
topPRs.body:
Stacked on #9844 (PR2 freeze).

PR3 of the seamless-provision handoff — the **invisible re-point**: make the shared→dedicated switch visually invisible (no full-screen `StartupScreen` flash, no wiped composer draft, no `SWITCH_AGENT`/coordinator re-entry).

## The problem
Today the handoff's `onSwitch` → `switchAgentProfile` → `clearAllChatDrafts` (wipes composer) + `SWITCH_AGENT` → coordinator `ready→polling-backend` (not shell-paintable) → `App.tsx:2057` swaps the shell for `<StartupScreen/>`; and `setBaseUrl` unconditionally `disconnectWs`. = full-screen flash + dropped WS + lost draft.

## How
- New `ElizaClient.repointBaseUrl(base)` — flips the base + persistence (extracted `persistBaseUrl`, byte-identical to `setBaseUrl`'s body) **without** the unconditional `disconnectWs`, swapping the WS in place. Global `setBaseUrl`/`disconnectWs` untouched.
- New `cloud/handoff/silent-repoint.ts` — persists the dedicated active-server + profile, `setToken`, `repointBaseUrl`. **NO** `clearAllChatDrafts`, **NO** `switchAgentProfile`, **NO** `SWITCH_AGENT`. Chat stays mounted on the same conversation id (transcript already copied by PR2's supervisor).
- The handoff `onSwitch` points at this silent path; every other caller keeps the global switch.
- Removed the shipped `isCloudAgentWaking` banner (+ its tests + sole consumer); the Settings row keeps its local resume chip; the chat progress toast (`CloudHandoffBanner`) stays.

## ⚠️ Known WARN (from /clean + /review — surfaced honestly)
The in-place WS-swap is currently **inert on cloud bases**: both the shared REST adapter and `*.elizacloud.ai` are treated as connected-without-WS, so `connectWs()` no-ops (`client-base.ts:1082`). So the ~40 lines of socket teardown are dead-weight on these bases today — they no-op safely (no crash, no flap). The re-point's real "invisible" wins (no `StartupScreen` flash, no draft clear, no `SWITCH_AGENT`, global path untouched) hold **independent** of the WS swap. **Reviewer's call** whether to prune the WS handling now or keep it for when a base does use WS.

## Safety + tests
Gated behind `preferSharedCloudTier` (default off → byte-identical; global `setBaseUrl`/`switchAgentProfile` unchanged for all other callers). 21 tests (silent-repoint + controller, incl. asserting the handoff switch does NOT clear drafts / does NOT dispatch `SWITCH_AGENT`) + `tsc` clean. Passed /clean + /review (No FAIL; 2 cleanups applied).

Not in scope: delete-shared (PR4), billing (PR5).

completedItems.title: fix(feed): complete Steward OAuth login (read code from URL fragment) + remove Farcaster
completedItems.body:
## Login bounce fix
Steward's PKCE callback returns the one-time exchange code in the URL **fragment** (`setRedirectFragment` → `url.hash`: `#code=…&state=…`) to keep it out of server logs / Referer / history. The feed callback page read it

topContributors.summary: standujar: Drove significant architectural improvements to the elizaos/eliza handoff system by implementing critical features for agent switching, queuing, and state management across seven PRs (#9843, #9844, #9845, #9846, #9854, #9856, #9857), while simultaneously streamlining the codebase through the removal of over 2,600 lines of legacy cloud and UI components (#9834, #9835, #9836). Their work today was primarily focused on feature development and bug fixes, with a secondary emphasis on refactoring and cleanup tasks.

odilitime: Focused on stabilizing the elizaos/eliza codebase by merging 8 pull requests that addressed critical bug fixes and environment optimizations, most notably resolving cloud connector settings in PR #9860. Their work primarily centered on bugfix efforts, including streamlining desktop development environments and refining core runtime imports.

NubsCarson: Drove critical multi-tenant hardening and security improvements for the elizaos/eliza cloud infrastructure, merging 12 PRs that addressed SSRF vulnerabilities (#9864), rate-limiting gaps (#9863), and tenant isolation (#9865). Their work focused primarily on bugfixes and testing, including the initiation of a comprehensive hardening tracking issue (#9853) to prepare the platform for general availability.

lalalune: Focused on improving authentication stability by resolving a critical issue with Steward OAuth login in elizaos/eliza (#9831). They also contributed to project maintenance through active engagement in PR and issue discussions.

noobshow: Focused on improving core system flexibility by merging PR #9839 in elizaos/eliza, which enables the use of TemplateType functions within CharacterInput. This contribution reflects a dedicated effort toward bugfix work within the core codebase.

digitalweb33333-creator: Expanded the ecosystem's integration capabilities by merging PR #9837 into elizaos/eliza, which added third-party plugin-x402-endpoints. This contribution focused entirely on configuration updates to enhance registry functionality.
